name: I hurt my back at work in Murray Utah but waited a week to tell my boss, is my claim ruined?
description: Report ASAP within 180 days in Utah; delays hurt but don't always bar claims if documented.[1][2]

mainEntity.name: I hurt my back at work in Murray Utah but waited a week to tell my boss, is my claim ruined?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report ASAP within 180 days in Utah; delays hurt but don't always bar claims if documented.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description: No, your claim isn't automatically ruined if reported within 180 days in Utah, but delays make it harder to prove work-relatedness and raise insurer suspicion.[1][2] Utah law requires notice 'as soon as practicable,' with failure after 180 days barring benefits entirely.[2] Key factors: Provide verifiable proof like email timestamps; employer must file Form 122 within 7 days; medical causation must link to work.[1][2] Practical guidance: Report now in writing, explain the delay honestly to your doctor, and gather witnesses if possible. An attorney can help rebut presumptions against late claims.
